title: Microsoft Spends a Bunch on Patents — But Is It Worth It?
author: Kevin Maney
abstract: USA Today's Kevin Maney calculates Microsoft spent ~$9.5M per patent in 2003 — far more than IBM ($2.5M), HP, or Sun. Maney questions whether $5B+ annual R&D yields breakthrough products. Peter Kastner of Aberdeen Group defends Microsoft Research as underappreciated. The piece foregrounds the disconnect between research output and commercial mega-hits beyond Windows and Office.
importance_rationale: Surfaces the early-2000s critique that Microsoft's R&D dollars under-converted to new platform hits — a thesis that aged well as Xbox, MSN, and Tablet PC under-performed until the cloud/AI era.
relevance_rationale: R&D-to-product yield ratios remain a recurring framework in tech analyst coverage; Microsoft Research's role still debated.
prescience_rationale: Maney's skepticism about Microsoft converting R&D into mega-hits proved correct for the 2004-2014 decade; only Azure (2010) and later AI/Copilot reversed the pattern.
